methodsAttempted.notes: Search identified the 2023 CUAED teaching recommendations, 2025 GAIA-GEN recommendations, the iagen.unam.mx student guide, and news or unit-level sources.
Search confirmed integrity and assessment language in official UNAM recommendation PDFs and also returned journal/event pages that were not used as university-level evidence.
Search surfaced the DGTIC ethics PDF and additional official but lower-specificity pages.
Fetched three official UNAM PDFs with Firecrawl and direct HTTP/PDF text extraction for hash-stable snapshots.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found three UNAM PDF sources suitable for narrow staged claims: the 2025 GAIA-GEN recommendations, the 2023 CUAED teaching recommendations, and a 2025 DGTIC ethics diagnostic. Official news, research, journal-specific, external-guide, and unit-level materials were rejected from claim extraction.

artifacts.claimText: UNAM's 2025 GAIA-GEN document frames its generative-AI education content as recommendations for informed, ethical, and critical use, not as a rigid prescriptive framework.

citation.citationTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNAM's 2025 education recommendations advise faculty using generative AI in teaching to avoid entering personal or sensitive information into AI platforms and to orient students and colleagues about data privacy.

citation.citationTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For assessment, UNAM's 2025 recommendations advise faculty to define the level of generative-AI incorporation and, when AI use is allowed, ask students to provide the prompts/instructions and generated responses for review.

citation.citationTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNAM's 2025 recommendations advise designing strategies for undeclared generative-AI use and caution against indiscriminate use of AI detectors because their effectiveness is questioned and they raise privacy concerns.

citation.citationTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UNAM's 2023 teaching recommendations suggest that faculty clearly specify acceptable and unacceptable ChatGPT use, require recognition and documentation of AI-tool use, and emphasize user responsibility for submitted academic products.

artifacts.claimText: A 2025 UNAM DGTIC ethics diagnostic says UNAM did not yet have an institutional framework for ethical integration of AI systems, while recommending stronger normative work and ethics-committee capacity for AI cases.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Estas recomendaciones aspiran a ser elementos que detonen en la comunidad universitaria (profesorado, estudiantado, funcionariado) creatividad y apropiación crítica de estas tecnologías, no pretenden ser marcos rígidos que intenten prescribir el trabajo académico respecto a la IAGen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: These recommendations are intended to encourage critical appropriation, not to be rigid frameworks prescribing academic work with GenAI.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Protección de datos y privacidad. Evita proporcionar información personal o sensible en plataformas de IAGen y asegura el cumplimiento de las normativas de privacidad. Orienta al estudiantado, así como a tus colegas, acerca de la importancia de la privacidad de los datos y cómo las distintas plataformas los procesan.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Data protection and privacy: avoid providing personal or sensitive information in GenAI platforms and orient students and colleagues about data privacy.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Recomendaciones para el uso educativo de la Inteligencia Artificial Generativa en la UNAM
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Definir su nivel de incorporación. Es importante determinar en qué medida utilizar la IAGen en función de los conocimientos y habilidades que se desean evaluar. ... En aquellos casos en los que se permita el uso de IAGen, solicita que el estudiantado proporcione las instrucciones y respuestas generadas por la herramienta para valorarlas.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Define the level of GenAI incorporation for the knowledge and skills being assessed; when allowed, ask students to provide prompts/instructions and generated responses.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Diseñar estrategias que prevengan y atiendan el uso no declarado de la IAGen. ... Evitar el uso indiscriminado de detectores de IAGen. Actualmente, su eficacia es cuestionada y plantea preocupaciones de privacidad.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Design strategies for undeclared GenAI use and avoid indiscriminate AI-detector use because effectiveness is questioned and privacy concerns exist.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Asimismo, establecer la necesidad de reconocer y documentar cómo se utilizó la herramienta y qué acciones se tomaron para decidir la permanencia o no del contenido creado por estas herramientas, así como enfatizar la responsabilidad final del usuario sobre los productos académicos que se entreguen a revisión.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Establish the need to recognize and document how the tool was used and emphasize the user's final responsibility for academic products submitted for review.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Uso y desarrollo ético de la Inteligencia Artificial en la Universidad: docencia e investigación
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Por el momento, la UNAM aún no cuenta con ningún marco institucional de integración de la ética en los SIA; no obstante existen áreas universitarias, como el IIMAS o el IIJ, que han profundizado en la aplicación de la ética al proceso investigativo y en la ética del derecho.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The diagnostic says UNAM does not yet have an institutional ethical-integration framework for AI systems, while noting university areas working on ethics and AI topics.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Sección 5.2.1, marcos de integración en la UNAM
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Uso y desarrollo ético de la Inteligencia Artificial en la Universidad: docencia e investigación
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Las implicaciones descritas ... están ligadas entre sí por la necesidad de transparentar el uso de los SIA y sus herramientas, lo que obliga moralmente al investigador a explicitar con claridad si se ha apoyado en ellas, ya sea en la formulación de preguntas, hipótesis y objetivos, en el diseño de experimentos, en la ejecución de los cálculos, el análisis de los datos o la elaboración del informe de investigación.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The document links AI-mediated research ethics to transparency: researchers should clearly state whether they used AI tools in research questions, design, calculations, analysis, or report writing.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Sección 5.4, integración de la ética en la investigación

artifacts.title: Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México staged AI-policy evidence draft
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source evidence for UNAM is based on Spanish UNAM PDFs from GAIA-GEN/CUAED and DGTIC. Claims are limited to recommendations and diagnostic findings on generative AI in education, privacy, academic integrity, assessment transparency, AI-detector caution, and research transparency.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged bundle does not publish canonical claims and does not treat recommendations or diagnostic observations as binding university-wide policy unless the source says so.
External-authored, news-only, research-only, journal-specific, and unit-level sources were rejected from claim extraction.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
